narration: You're paying for a gym you haven't visited in months. A streaming service you forgot exists. A stock you know is dead. But you can't stop — and there's a name for why.
visual: Dark background. Person silhouette at a cobweb-covered gym door, credit card floating nearby with recurring charge animation. Bold headline: PAYING FOR NOTHING?

narration: It's called the sunk cost fallacy. Your brain refuses to accept a loss, so it keeps throwing good money after bad. It affects 78% of people — and you're probably one of them.
visual: Light background. SVG brain with dollar coins draining out the bottom. Text overlay: SUNK COST FALLACY. Counter animating to 78% with a red progress bar.

narration: Take gyms. The average unused membership costs $600 a year. And 67% of members don't exercise regularly — they're literally paying monthly just to feel guilty.
visual: Dark background. Gym building SVG with a chain-and-lock on the door. Calendar with red X marks across 6 months. Money bag shrinking. Text: $600/YEAR GONE.

narration: Same thing in investing. People hold losing stocks 60% longer than winning ones. Your brain says if I sell, the loss becomes real — but here's the thing, it's already real.
visual: Light background. Stock chart SVG trending sharply down. Person figure gripping the chart line with both hands. Red arrow pointing down labeled: 60% LONGER.

narration: Add it all up — the gym, the dead subscriptions, the bad positions you're sitting on. The average person loses $4,700 every single year to sunk cost thinking.
visual: Dark background. Cracked piggy bank SVG with coins leaking out. Three labeled icons below: GYM, SUBSCRIPTIONS, STOCKS. Dollar counter animating up to $4,700.

narration: The fix is one question: if I didn't already own this, would I buy it today? If the answer is no — cut it now. Your future self gets to keep that $4,700.
visual: Light background. Two-path fork road SVG: left path labeled KEEP (LOSE) in red, right path labeled CUT (WIN) in green. Bold question text above. Follow CTA at bottom.
